title: pilosella vulpina
intro: Pilosella vulpina, also known as mouse-ear hawk's-beard or yellow hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Pilosella vulpina has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects. Pilosella vulpina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and dried flowers, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
